proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients with feature_014=1 vs feature_014=0 (any direction).
Mean pfs_months differs between patients with feature_027=1 vs feature_027=0.
Mean pfs_months differs across the three levels of feature_001 (0, 1, 2).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_014']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_014']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1 (n=27481) mean pfs_months 3.70 vs feature_014=0 (n=22519) mean 5.05; difference -1.35 months (t-test p<1e-300).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_027']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_027']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_027=1 (n=10022) mean pfs_months 5.09 vs feature_027=0 (n=39978) mean 4.12; difference +0.97 months (t-test p<1e-300).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_001')['pfs_months'].mean(); ANOVA F-test
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0 mean 5.27 (n=17592); feature_001=1 mean 4.05 (n=24971); feature_001=2 mean 2.91 (n=7437). Strong monotonic decline; ANOVA highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months across the cohort.
feature_023=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_023=0.
feature_006=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0.
feature_022=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_022=0.
feature_011=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_011=0.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.766 (p<1e-300). Mean pfs_months rises monotonically from 0.26 in the 30-36 bin to 8.41 in the 84-90 bin. Striking positive association.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_023']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_023']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 (n=20898) mean 4.12 vs feature_023=0 (n=29102) mean 4.45; -0.33 months, p=8.5e-56.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1 (n=17657) mean 4.11 vs feature_006=0 (n=32343) mean 4.42; -0.31 months, p=5.2e-46.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on feature_022
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=1 (n=2272) mean 4.09 vs feature_022=0 (n=47728) mean 4.32; -0.23 months, p=3.7e-6.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on feature_011
analyses.result_summary: feature_011=1 (n=1506) mean 4.53 vs feature_011=0 (n=48494) mean 4.30; +0.22 months, p=2.3e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous lab-like features (feature_005, feature_019, feature_021, feature_025, feature_032, feature_017, feature_003, feature_030, feature_008, feature_004, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_007, feature_033, feature_009) are individually correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: stats.pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Strongest unadjusted correlations: feature_025 r=-0.123 (p=2e-167); feature_019 r=+0.102 (p=3e-115); feature_005 r=-0.038 (p=3e-17); feature_021 r=-0.011 (p=0.013). Other 12 continuous features: |r|<0.013, mostly non-significant. Most continuous features show only weak unadjusted univariate signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_015, feature_001, and the other binary features, the negative association of feature_014 with pfs_months persists with effect approximately -1.35 months.
After multivariable adjustment, the positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months persists with effect roughly +0.93 months.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_011's positive marginal effect attenuates and is no longer significant (i.e., it is confounded by other covariates).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_031 + feature_014 + feature_006 + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_022 + feature_016 + feature_028 + feature_002 + feature_024 + feature_010 + feature_012 + feature_018 + feature_020 + feature_027', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: feature_014 = -1.354 (p<1e-300); feature_027 = +0.935 (p<1e-300); feature_023 = -0.355; feature_006 = -0.281; feature_022 = -0.361; feature_011 = +0.010 (p=0.70, NS after adjustment). feature_015 = +0.176 per unit. feature_001 levels 1,2 = -1.20, -2.33. R^2=0.830.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment, feature_019 has a positive partial effect on pfs_months and feature_025 has a negative partial effect; smaller adjusted effects are present for feature_005 and feature_021.

analyses.code: smf.ols with all features included
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted: feature_019 = +0.469 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_025 = -0.076 per unit (p<1e-300), feature_005 = -0.005 per unit (p=1e-97), feature_021 = -0.0004 per unit (p=6e-19). All other 12 continuous features non-significant after adjustment. R^2 increases to 0.859.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 acts as a treatment that improves pfs_months overall but its effect is not constant across patient subgroups; at least one binary feature should show a significant feature_027-by-feature interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for f in features: ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*f + base_adjusters'); collect interaction p-values.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions (adjusted): feature_027 x feature_006 = -1.46 (p<1e-300); feature_027 x feature_023 = -1.63 (p<1e-300); feature_027 x feature_005 = -0.026 per unit (p<1e-300); feature_027 x feature_022 = -0.99 (p=8e-104); feature_027 x feature_011 = +0.89 (p=2e-57). Multiple highly significant interactions confirm strong heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 confers no measurable benefit in patients with feature_006=1; the entire main effect of feature_027 is concentrated in feature_006=0 patients.
feature_027 confers no measurable benefit in patients with feature_023=1; the entire main effect is concentrated in feature_023=0 patients.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_027 within feature_006 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: tx (n=6524) 5.83 vs ctrl (n=25819) 4.35; diff=+1.48, p<1e-300. feature_006=1: tx (n=3498) 4.13 vs ctrl (n=14159) 4.10; diff=+0.03, p=0.52. Treatment effect vanishes when feature_006=1.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_027 within feature_023 levels
analyses.result_summary: feature_023=0: tx (n=5816) 5.74 vs ctrl (n=23286) 4.08; diff=+1.66, p<1e-300. feature_023=1: tx (n=4206) 4.13 vs ctrl (n=16692) 4.10; diff=+0.03, p=0.45. Treatment effect vanishes when feature_023=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full benefit of feature_027 on pfs_months is concentrated in the joint subgroup where feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0; outside that subgroup the treatment effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of feature_027 within the 2x2 of feature_006 and feature_023
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0 & feature_023=0: tx (n=3763) 6.64 vs ctrl (n=15090) 4.12, diff=+2.52, p<1e-300. feature_006=0 & feature_023=1: diff=+0.06, p=0.17. feature_006=1 & feature_023=0: diff=+0.07, p=0.17. feature_006=1 & feature_023=1: diff=-0.04, p=0.52. The +2.5 month signal is exclusive to the (0,0) cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0), low feature_005 (specifically feature_005<5) further enhances the feature_027 treatment benefit, while feature_005>=5 abolishes it.

analyses.code: Within the (006=0,023=0) subgroup, stratify feature_027 effect by feature_005 quartile and by progressive cutpoints.
analyses.result_summary: Q1 (feature_005 0.04-1.97): tx 8.78 vs ctrl 4.14, diff=+4.64. Q2 (1.97-4.49): diff=+4.65. Q3 (4.49-9.95): diff=+0.55. Q4 (9.95-442): diff=-0.03. A sharp threshold at feature_005~=5 separates large benefit from no benefit; cut at 5 gives diff +4.625 (n=10100) vs -0.016 (n=8753).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005<5) subgroup, the rare feature_022=1 status further suppresses the feature_027 effect to zero.

analyses.code: Within the (006=0,023=0,005<5) subgroup, stratify feature_027 effect by feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: feature_022=0 (n=9326): tx (n=1903) 9.10 vs ctrl (n=7423) 4.09; diff=+5.01, p<1e-300. feature_022=1 (n=774): tx 3.96 vs ctrl 4.14; diff=-0.17, p=0.35. feature_022=1 abolishes the otherwise +5 month effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The composite treatment-responsive subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_005<5 AND feature_022=0 captures essentially all of the feature_027 benefit; outside this subgroup the effect is null.

analyses.code: Compare feature_027 effect inside vs outside the composite subgroup; final OLS with composite x feature_027 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Inside composite (n=9326, 18.7%): tx 9.10 vs ctrl 4.09, diff=+5.01, p<1e-300. Outside (n=40674): diff=+0.025, p=0.33. Interaction in full adjusted model feature_027 x composite = +4.98 (p<1e-300); main feature_027 (in composite=0) = -0.003 (p=0.26). The composite subgroup is necessary and sufficient: outside it, feature_027 has no effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the composite responsive subgroup, no remaining single feature acts as a categorical effect modifier of comparable magnitude to the four predicates already identified.

analyses.code: Within the composite subgroup, screen feature_027 x f interactions for all remaining features.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest within-composite interactions are small adjustments rather than categorical modifiers: feature_027 x feature_015 = +0.005 per unit (p=1e-36); feature_027 x feature_001(level2) = -0.07 (p=1e-7); feature_027 x feature_014 = -0.028 (p=0.001). All are small relative to the +5.0 month main subgroup effect; no further binary turns the effect on/off.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 is positively associated with pfs_months mainly because it overlaps with the responsive subgroup (feature_011=1 implies feature_023=0 and feature_022=0); after conditioning on the composite predicates the marginal feature_011 effect becomes irrelevant.

analyses.code: Cross-tabs of feature_011 with feature_023 and feature_022; within-composite test for feature_027 x feature_011 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: All 1506 patients with feature_011=1 have feature_023=0 and feature_022=0 (perfect implication). Within composite subgroup, feature_027 x feature_011 interaction p=0.31 (NS). Thus feature_011's marginal positive effect is structural overlap with composite predicates rather than independent benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 has a near-constant prognostic detrimental effect (~ -1.35 months) that does not interact strongly with the candidate treatment feature_027.

analyses.code: Adjusted ols with feature_027*feature_014 interaction; full screen of feature_014*f interactions.
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 x feature_014 = +0.034 (p=0.08, NS). Strongest feature_014 interaction is with feature_015 (-0.0069 per unit, p=9e-19); next with feature_001 (small). feature_014's main effect (-1.37 in final model) is essentially constant across treatment status - it acts as a prognostic factor rather than a treatment effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 has a strong, approximately linear positive partial effect on pfs_months (~+0.18 months per unit) that persists after full adjustment, dominating prognosis.

analyses.code: Final OLS coefficient from full multivariable model.
analyses.result_summary: feature_015 adjusted slope = +0.176 (95% CI 0.175-0.177, p<1e-300). Range 30 to 90 implies an end-to-end shift of about +10.6 months in pfs_months, dwarfing all other prognostic factors. Direction is opposite to a conventional age effect (older = longer PFS), suggesting feature_015 is not simply chronological age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 ordinal levels (0/1/2) show a monotone decline in pfs_months consistent with a stage-like prognostic gradient; level 2 confers about -2.3 months relative to level 0 after adjustment.

analyses.code: Final OLS with C(feature_001).
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_001 contrasts: level 1 = -1.19 (p<1e-300); level 2 = -2.32 (p<1e-300) relative to level 0. Monotone descending gradient consistent with an ordered severity / stage-like coding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) has a positive adjusted effect on pfs_months consistent with a favorable lab marker (e.g., albumin-like), with each unit adding ~0.49 months.
feature_025 (continuous, range 0-24, mean ~3.8) has a negative adjusted effect on pfs_months (~-0.08 months per unit) consistent with an unfavorable cumulative-exposure-like measure.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_019 in full OLS.
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 adjusted slope = +0.469 (p<1e-300). Across its 1.5-5.5 range that translates to roughly +1.9 months end-to-end.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_025 in full OLS.
analyses.result_summary: feature_025 adjusted slope = -0.076 per unit (p<1e-300). Over 0-24 range implies up to -1.8 months end-to-end.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 has both a small overall-adjusted negative association with pfs_months and a stronger role as a treatment-effect modifier of feature_027, consistent with a tumor-burden-like marker that both worsens prognosis and predicts non-response.

analyses.code: Adjusted main effect of feature_005 plus interaction with feature_027.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effect: -0.005 per unit (p=1e-97). Interaction with feature_027: -0.026 per unit (p<1e-300). The interaction magnitude dominates: high feature_005 abolishes feature_027 benefit even where feature_006=0 and feature_023=0, supporting a dual prognostic+predictive role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most other binary features (feature_010, feature_012, feature_016, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_028, feature_031, feature_002) show no detectable independent effect on pfs_months after full multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: Full OLS, examine these coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients all |b|<0.04 with p>0.10: feature_031 p=0.44, feature_010 p=0.45, feature_012 p=0.68, feature_016 p=0.67, feature_018 p=0.41, feature_020 p=0.12, feature_024 p=0.14, feature_028 p=0.23, feature_002 p=0.97. Treated as null prognostic features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous features, after full adjustment, feature_032, feature_017, feature_007, feature_003, feature_008, feature_013, feature_029, feature_030, feature_033, feature_004, feature_026, feature_009 contribute essentially no additional prognostic information for pfs_months.

analyses.code: Full OLS coefficients for these continuous features.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted slopes near zero with p>0.05 for all 12 listed continuous features (p ranging 0.06 to 0.92). Once feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_005, feature_019, feature_025, feature_027 and feature_022 are in the model, no additional continuous feature adds detectable signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An additional joint model that includes feature_027 x composite captures essentially all the heterogeneity of the treatment effect; the within-composite treatment effect (after adjusting for feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025) is approximately +5.0 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*composite + feature_015 + C(feature_001) + feature_014 + feature_019 + feature_025', data=df) where composite = (feature_006==0)&(feature_023==0)&(feature_005<5)&(feature_022==0).
analyses.result_summary: feature_027 main (composite=0): -0.003 (p=0.26). composite main: -0.005 (p=0.05). feature_027:composite = +4.981 (p<1e-300). Adjusted final estimate of treatment effect within composite: +4.98 months (95% CI 4.97-4.99). Outside composite: 0 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the composite subgroup, the treatment-effect magnitude grows weakly with feature_015 (+0.005 month/unit additional) but the effect is positive across the entire feature_015 range; thus feature_015 is a quantitative modifier, not a categorical gate.

analyses.code: Within composite subgroup, ols('pfs_months ~ feature_027*feature_015 + ...').
analyses.result_summary: feature_027:feature_015 = +0.0054 (p=1e-36). Predicted treatment effect at feature_015=40 ~ +4.7 months; at feature_015=80 ~ +5.0 months. Direction always positive; feature_015 modulates magnitude but does not eliminate benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among rare binary features (feature_011 3%, feature_022 4.5%, feature_016 5%, feature_028 3%, feature_002 0.5%), only feature_022 acts as a meaningful effect modifier of feature_027 after adjusting for other features in the composite responder definition.

analyses.code: Adjusted feature_027 x feature_X interactions for the rare binaries within the (006=0 & 023=0 & 005<5) subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Within (006=0 & 023=0 & 005<5): feature_027 x feature_022 = -2.74 (p<1e-300, abolishes effect); feature_027 x feature_011 = +0.37 (small additional, p=4e-5); feature_027 x feature_016 NS (p=0.59); feature_027 x feature_028 NS (p=0.27); feature_027 x feature_002 NS (p=0.24). feature_022 confirmed as a categorical 'switch-off' modifier in the composite subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-way and 3-way subgroup search using the four composite predicates does not reveal a 5th binary predicate of similar magnitude; the composite (feature_006=0 & feature_023=0 & feature_022=0 & feature_005<5) is approximately the maximal disjunctive description of the responsive subgroup.

analyses.code: Iterate through every remaining feature within the composite subgroup; test feature_027 x f interactions for any p<0.001 with magnitude >0.5 month.
analyses.result_summary: No remaining feature-with-feature_027 interaction within the composite subgroup exceeds 0.15 month in absolute magnitude. The dominant within-subgroup signal is the +5 month on/off response to feature_027; the composite definition is essentially exhaustive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final composite hypothesis: In patients with feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5 (about 18.7% of the cohort, n=9326), receiving treatment feature_027 increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months relative to not receiving feature_027; in the complementary 81.3% of the cohort, feature_027 has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: T-test of feature_027 within composite vs outside; final adjusted OLS interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: Composite subgroup (n=9326): tx mean 9.10 vs ctrl 4.09, unadjusted diff +5.01 (p<1e-300); adjusted diff +4.98 (p<1e-300). Complement (n=40674): tx mean 4.15 vs ctrl 4.12, diff +0.025 (p=0.33). The composite hypothesis is fully supported as the maximal subgroup definition for feature_027 efficacy on pfs_months.
